Ad Text:
Job Title: Operational Readiness Lead
PayRate: $97.00ph
Location: Cockburn
Work Hours: Monday - Friday - 38hr per week
Work From Home: Up to 1 day per week
Client: WA State Governement
Employment Status: Casual Contract
Duration: 3 months with chance of extension
Start Date: ASAP


The Opportunity

Are you a Change and Readiness specialist who thrives in the "final sprint" of a major system implementation?

We are seeking a high-caliber Operational Readiness Lead to join a critical State Government project: the delivery of MyFinance, a new Financial Management Information System (FMIS) based on TechnologyOne. With the project in its final stages, your mission is to ensure the organisation is fully prepared to transition from project delivery to Business-as-Usual (BAU).

The Role

Reporting to the Project Manager, you will be the primary bridge between the technical project team and the broader business. You will lead the charge in stakeholder engagement, training coordination, and transition planning.

Key Responsibilities

Stakeholder Alignment: Facilitate communication across Finance and multidisciplinary business areas to ensure go-live transparency.

Training Coordination: Identify participant groups, align training with project milestones, and support solution demonstrations/knowledge transfer.

UAT Readiness: Ensure business participants are fully prepped for User Acceptance Testing, understanding their specific roles and expectations.

Operating Model Design: Assist in defining the future state for the MyFinance Systems team, including post-go-live capability and training requirements.

About You

You are a seasoned change professional with a foot in both the ICT and Finance camps. You understand the complexities of a financial system rollout and possess the "soft skills" to navigate diverse stakeholder needs.

Essential Criteria:

Experience: 5+ years of proven experience leading change management and operational readiness for complex ICT or Finance system implementations.

Communication: Exceptional facilitation skills with the ability to deliver presentations and workshops to diverse audiences.

Engagement: Demonstrated success in developing and executing comprehensive stakeholder engagement plans.

Strategy: Strong analytical skills to define future operating models and manage competing project priorities.

Desirable:

Prior experience with TechnologyOne (TechOne) or TFMIS.

Experience within State Government or large-scale Finance transformations in the private sector.

Why Apply?

This is a high-visibility role within a major government department. It offers a competitive hourly rate and the opportunity to add a successful, large-scale FMIS implementation to your portfolio.

The role is highly modelling focused and will suit someone with strong Big 4 modelling or related advisory experience who enjoys detailed model review, reconciliation, assumption validation, scenario analysis, and building more robust, transparent, and repeatable modelling frameworks.

Our client is a leading Australian infrastructure investment business with exposure to regulated infrastructure assets. 
 
Responsibilities

Support the development of a more consistent modelling framework and governance approach
Assist with rebaselining models in line with regulatory determinations, macroeconomic assumptions, and securityholder positions
Run sensitivity analysis and scenario testing across key value drivers

Requirements

2+ years of relevant experience in Big 4 modelling or a related advisory environment
Advanced financial modelling and analytical skills, with experience in financial forecasting
Strong Excel capability and intermediate to advanced PowerPoint skills
Degree qualified in finance, accounting, commerce, economics, mathematics, or a related discipline
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret complex financial data
Strong attention to detail, with the ability to identify and articulate data patterns
Excellent verbal communication skills, with the ability to be concise and liaise with people at all levels
Strong written communication skills, with the ability to produce clear presentations and reports
Positive attitude and strong teamwork skills, with the ability to work effectively in a small team
Experience with regulated assets is highly desirable

Job Title: Principal / Director - Data Privacy & DPIA | Senior Leadership
Ad Text:
Our agency is proud to exclusively represent a global leader in cybersecurity and compliance services as they expand their Privacy & Data Protection Consulting practice across Australia. We are seeking a senior Data Privacy & DPIA Subject Matter Expert to take on a market-facing leadership role, combining high-level consulting delivery with strategic business development.

Based in Perth with a Hybrid work structure, this is a pivotal opportunity to build and lead a scalable privacy consulting business at a national level.


The Role

This position is designed for a senior professional who thrives at the intersection of executive advisory and commercial growth. You will be responsible for:
Consulting & Advisory: Leading and delivering Data Privacy Impact Assessments (DPIA/PIA) for enterprise and government clients.
Executive Influence: Acting as a trusted advisor to Boards, CIOs, CISOs, and Legal/Risk leaders on privacy governance and regulatory readiness.
Business Development: Driving pre-sales through client discovery workshops, solution design, and SoW development.
Strategic Growth: Developing the firm's service portfolio, methodologies, and templates to establish the practice as a "go-to" firm in the Australian market.



What We're Looking For

Experience: 10+ years within data privacy, regulatory compliance, cybersecurity, or legal advisory.
Background: A Big 4 or Tier-1 consulting background is highly preferred (Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG or equivalent).

Commercial Prowess: Proven ability to engage C-suite stakeholders and work alongside sales teams to convert pipelines.
Career Growth: The client is open to 'bridge' candidates-ambitious leaders ready to step up into their first Director role within a new market sector

Preferred Qualifications

Candidates holding the following certifications will be highly regarded:

IAPP: CIPP/A, CIPP/E, CIPM, or CIPT.

Standards: ISO/IEC 27701 or ISO/IEC 27001 (Lead Implementer/Auditor).


This is a rare opportunity to join an expanding global firm and shape their privacy service offering from the ground up.

About the company

Tyton Ecological Intelligence builds AI-powered tools for large-scale ecological monitoring. Mining companies, land managers, and ecologists use our products to monitor vegetation, assess rehabilitation, and support biodiversity compliance, all driven by remote sensing and spatial data. We're a small, tight-knit team based in Perth, working on problems that don't have obvious off-the-shelf answers.

About the role

We're looking for an Ecological Data Scientist to strengthen the analytical foundations of the platform. The role sits at the intersection of ecology, statistics, and data science: applying rigorous methods to large-scale environmental datasets, integrating remote sensing outputs with field data, and building frameworks that make ecological monitoring more consistent and repeatable.

What you'll do

You’ll be part of the R&D team, working on the analytical capabilities that power Tyton’s products. The methods and frameworks you develop feed directly into TytonAI and TytonIQ as production features, used by ecologists and land managers in the field.

Apply rigorous ecological statistical analyses to large-scale environmental datasets

Develop and validate analytical frameworks that underpin new platform capabilities

Integrate remote sensing outputs with field-based ecological data to improve model inputs and outputs

Work with spatial and time-series datasets to detect trends and variability at landscape scale

Build predictive and explanatory models for ecological outcomes

Translate analytical approaches into documented, reproducible methods that can be implemented in production systems

Collaborate closely with machine learning engineers and ecologists to take ideas from concept through to deployed features

Support data quality, validation, and interpretation across multiple datasets

What we're looking for

PhD in ecology or a related scientific field

Strong experience applying ecological statistics to large datasets (e.g. experimental design, multivariate analysis, time-series analysis, modelling)

Experience with spatial statistics and analysis

Proficiency in R and/or Python (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, scikit-learn or similar)

Ability to work both independently and collaboratively on complex, open-ended problems

Nice to have

Experience using Python for data analysis workflows

Exposure to machine learning in environmental applications

Experience with GIS (Q, ESRI)

Why join

The problems here are genuinely interesting. You'll be working with real ecological data at scale, in a domain where rigorous analysis has direct environmental impact. The team is small and experienced, which means your work feeds directly into the platform rather than disappearing into a pipeline. If you want to do serious analytical work on hard ecological problems, this is a good place to do it!

Supporting month‑end close processes and ensuring all outputs are robust, credible and audit‑ready.
  
About You

Strong technical capability, including advanced Excel and financial modelling skills, with a practical, improvement mindset.
A hands‑on approach with the ability to move between detail and big‑picture commercial thinking.
Strong commercial judgement and curiosity, with the ability to connect operational drivers to financial outcomes.
Excellent stakeholder management skills, influencing non‑finance leaders with clear, pragmatic insights.
High attention to detail combined with pace, resilience and comfort working with ambiguity.
A strong sense of accountability and ownership, operating with credibility at senior levels.

Eastern Suburbs, Sydney – close to trains, buses and amenities
Circa $130,000–$150,000 + super (depending on experience)

Are you energised by turning complex information into clear priorities that enable better decisions?

 

DCCEEW is seeking a Senior Advisor, Strategy & Planning to play a pivotal role in coordinating and guiding how we plan, prioritise and invest in our digital, data and IT initiatives across the department.

 

As the Senior Advisor, Strategy & Planning, you will lead the coordination of DCCEEW’s IT investment planning and project prioritisation processes. You’ll provide strategic advice to senior stakeholders, support robust governance, and bring together complex inputs to create a clear, evidence‑based view of our investment pipeline.

 

You will also play a key role in shaping and embedding our strategy portfolio:

Data and digital strategy
IT Strategy
AI strategy

 

This will include enhancing transparency, improving decision‑making and driving better outcomes across the organisation. You will be involved in refreshing these key artefacts over time.

 

About You

 

You’re a confident, analytical professional who can see the big picture while managing detail. You’re comfortable working across diverse stakeholder groups, explaining complex concepts clearly, and influencing outcomes through strong relationships and evidence‑based insights.

 

You’ll bring:

Experience in IT strategy, planning or investment prioritisation, ideally in a complex environment.
2-3 yrs Management consulting / Advisory experience
A solid understanding of project management principles, governance and reporting.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor messages for different audiences.
A proactive, organised approach and a commitment to delivering high‑quality outcomes.

 

Essential Requirements 

Tertiary qualification in a relevant discipline and/or equivalent relevant experience
Mid – Advanced level proficiency across the MS365 tools stack ( PPT,EXCEL, Power BI, MS Lists ) 

 

For more information read the full Role Description: Senior Advisor, Strategy & Planning

 

Who we are

 

The Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW) works to protect the state’s environment and heritage. It leads the way on climate change, driving the sustainable transition to a net zero economy, powered by affordable, reliable, and clean energy.

 

DCCEEW conserves and protects the state’s natural environment. It manages the NSW national park estate, including its rich and diverse biodiversity and Aboriginal cultural heritage for future generations.

 

DCCEEW also ensures sustainable management of water resources across the state, to support the environment, communities and industry.

 

We acknowledge the ongoing custodial responsibilities of the Aboriginal peoples of NSW to care for Country and water and are committed to establishing meaningful partnerships with Aboriginal peoples in the management of the environment.

 

To Apply

 

If excited by the information above, we look forward to receiving your application, including a copy of your resume and cover letter expressing your interest and suitability for the role.

 

Please note to be eligible to apply for this position you must be an Australian or New Zealand citizen or permanent resident, or hold a valid Australian working Visa for the duration of the appointment. 

 

Applications close Tuesday 12 May at 11:55PM. 

 

Should you require further information about the role please contact Mike Lafford via mike.lafford@dcceew.nsw.gov.au

General Manager, Data, AI & Analytics

Location: Melbourne
Reports to: Executive Leadership

Talenza is partnering with a prestigious national brand to appoint a General Manager, Data, AI & Analytics. This is a pivotal leadership role focused on building capability, not just delivering outputs. This is less about dashboards, more about transformation.

The Opportunity

This role sits at the centre of enterprise enablement. Establish and embed responsible AI frameworks, governance and ethical standards.
Identify and prioritise high-value AI use cases with measurable outcomes.
Uplift organisation-wide data and AI literacy across leaders and teams.
Build and lead high-performing, multidisciplinary teams across data, analytics and AI.
Ensure strong data governance, quality, security and compliance.

What Success Looks Like
Organisation-wide trust and confidence in data.
Strong adoption of self-service analytics.
AI embedded in day-to-day decision-making.
Clear, effective AI governance and risk controls.
Tangible business value realised from AI initiatives.

About You
Proven senior leadership experience across data, analytics or AI in large, complex environments.
Strong understanding of modern data platforms, analytics and AI ecosystems.
Track record of driving enterprise-wide transformation through data.
Clear and pragmatic perspective on responsible and ethical AI.
Exceptional stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence at executive level.
Experience enabling multiple business domains through shared capability.

Company Overview

Excellent Trading, Investments, Front Office and/or Equities knowledge
Degree in Business, Commerce, Finance, Accounting and / or similar

What’s on Offer

Apply to Dave Marshall (David.marshall@profusiongroup.com) via the link today.

Profusion respect people, value diversity and are committed to equality. We are committed to providing a supportive culture and positively contributing towards creating diverse and inclusive workplaces for our candidates & clients. We invite candidates of all ages, genders, sexual orientation, cultural backgrounds, people with disability, neurodiverse individuals, and Indigenous Australians to apply.

Role Overview

This role sits within a growing Applications team and is responsible for delivering and supporting D365 Finance across both project and operational environments. On a daily basis, you will work directly with Finance stakeholders to translate requirements into system configuration across core modules including AP, AR, GL and project accounting. You will take ownership of data structures, financial dimensions and system behaviour, ensuring the platform accurately supports business operations.

Beyond day to day delivery, you will play a key role in shaping how Finance operates within D365. This includes improving financial processes, supporting month end and ensuring data flows cleanly into reporting platforms. You will work across integrations using standard frameworks, helping ensure data moves reliably across systems without unnecessary complexity or customisation.

This is a true techno functional role where you are expected to operate as a hands on owner rather than a coordinator. You will troubleshoot issues across configuration, data and integrations, working independently while engaging technical teams where required. The focus is on delivering outcomes that improve accuracy, compliance and efficiency across Finance, rather than purely technical outputs.

Responsibilities

Configure and optimise D365 Finance modules including P2P, AP, AR, GL and project accounting
Own financial structures including chart of accounts, dimensions and data setup
Translate Finance requirements directly into system configuration without reliance on developers
Support month end, financial operations and critical business processes
Troubleshoot issues across configuration, data and integrations, identifying root causes
Work with standard D365 integration frameworks including Data Management Framework and Electronic Reporting
Support and validate data flows into reporting layers including Power BI environments
Collaborate with Finance stakeholders including CFO, controllers and operational teams
Contribute to project delivery including enhancements, testing and deployments
Reduce reliance on external vendors by bringing capability in house

Candidate Profile

You are a hands on D365 Finance specialist who is comfortable operating across both functional and technical domains. You have strong experience configuring core Finance modules and understand how financial processes translate into system behaviour. Rather than sitting purely in analysis, you take ownership of delivery and are confident working directly in the system to drive outcomes.

You bring strong Finance domain knowledge, with experience across key processes such as procure to pay, receivables, general ledger and financial reporting. You are comfortable engaging directly with senior Finance stakeholders, understanding their requirements and translating them into practical system solutions without the need for a traditional BA layer.

Technically, you have a solid understanding of D365 data structures, integrations and troubleshooting. You can work across data entities, imports and system behaviour, and have enough exposure to X++ to understand and frame technical issues when required. You are pragmatic, delivery focused and thrive in environments where ownership, accountability and outcomes are valued.

Company, Culture & Benefits

This organisation is undergoing a significant transformation, modernising its technology landscape and investing heavily in D365 as a core platform. The environment is fast paced and operational, with a strong focus on delivery, ownership and continuous improvement. You will be part of a collaborative team where your impact is visible and valued.

Play a key role in a large scale ERP transformation
Hybrid working model with flexibility built in
Clear pathway to become a D365 Finance subject matter expert

Apply

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply. To apply please click apply or call Phillip Yee on +61 2 82893143 for a confidential discussion.

Highlights

Shape IT strategy with C-suite clients up to 1000 seats
Real autonomy to build your role during exciting global merger expansion
10 paid study days yearly, specialised training and growth opps

Position summary



It usually starts like this.

You’re grabbing a coffee somewhere in Sydney, pretending you’re just “checking emails,” when in reality you’re solving three client problems, calming down a fourth, and wondering if there’s a role out there where you could actually shape things instead of just reacting to them.

Good news, there is.

This role is for someone who’s a bit tired of being “the account manager” or “the consultant” and would quite like to be both, properly.


About the company



First Focus is Australia’s #1 mid-market Managed Service Provider, and yes, Cloudtango has now told us that nine years in a row. At this point it’s less “award-winning” and more “we should probably clear some shelf space.”

We’re now pushing close to 400 people globally, and just when things were getting interesting, we’ve announced our merger with Integris. Which means we’re about to become one of the largest and broadest SMB MSPs on the planet.

No pressure.

What that actually means for you is simple: bigger clients, broader capability, more opportunity, and a front-row seat to something genuinely exciting, without losing the culture that got us here in the first place.

We’re still a business where people know your name, where leadership is accessible, and where being informed, aligned, supported, and rewarded isn’t just a slide in a deck, it’s how things actually run day to day. You can read more about how we think about that on our About Us page at firstfocus dot com dot au ... check out the values there. And yep, we know everyone has these, but here we hire by them, reward by them, and make business decisions using them.


About the role



You’ll operate as a trusted advisor and Enterprise Account Manager, working with clients a range of clients, some approaching 1000 seats, partnering directly with senior stakeholders and the C-suite. You won’t just manage relationships, you’ll shape them.

The way this role plays out is not fixed. There's no dusty playbook to follow. You get to build this role in a way that actually makes sense, for you and for your clients.

You’ll help organisations navigate complexity, make smarter decisions, and occasionally stop them from buying something they absolutely do not need (they’ll thank you later).

If you’ve ever wanted to have a bigger impact, this is where you stop saying it and start doing it.


About the candidate



We’ll keep this refreshingly simple:

Proven success in a senior role within an MSP, MSSP, integrator, or similar
Experience working with enterprise clients and selling into the C-suite
A strong track record in account management and growth, you know how to build and retain trust
Confidence in consulting across IT strategy, infrastructure, and security
The ability to challenge constructively, communicate clearly, and follow through
Bonus points for a sense of humour and a low tolerance for unnecessary complexity.
Benefits and perks



Why You’ll Love It Here:

You’ll have real autonomy, not the “empowered within strict boundaries” kind
You’ll work with enterprise clients who expect, and value, proper thinking
You’ll help shape strategy, not just report on it
You’ll be part of a business that’s growing fast, but still knows how to laugh at itself
You’ll join at a moment where things are genuinely changing, in a good way




Plus:

Competitive salary package including up to $170k + super, 100% salary, 0% commission
Free access to Uprise, including 1:1 coaching with qualified psychologists or counsellors
Flexible hybrid working for work-life balance
“Never Stop Growing”, 10 paid study days per year and real motivation to grow and learn
Regular internal training and access to a learning library
FastTrack mentoring program
MAD Council, focused on equality, charity, and the environment
Tesla company car option (conditions apply)
Regular social events and a genuinely great team culture
How to apply



First Focus welcomes applications from Neurodiverse candidates. We recommend & appreciate disclosure along with the application so that a copy of our Neurodiversity Statement can be provided outlining the flexible options we can offer to best showcase your skills.

A high‑performing, collegiate investment team

A culture that values intellectual curiosity, evidence‑based decision‑making and accountability

The ability to influence firm‑wide investment strategy

This is a senior, hands-on leadership role responsible for building and scaling enterprise AI capability. As Lead Data Engineer, you'll design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning solutions that deliver tangible business outcomes across complex operational environments.




You'll work closely with data leaders, architects, and business stakeholders to identify opportunities and bring high-impact AI use cases to life-from predictive models through to cutting-edge generative AI solutions.




Key responsibilities include:





Leading end-to-end AI/ML solution design, development, and deployment
Driving innovation across NLP, LLMs, and advanced ML techniques
Establishing and scaling MLOps practices (CI/CD, monitoring, lifecycle management)
Building on modern cloud and data platforms (Azure, Databricks)
Embedding AI into enterprise systems to improve efficiency and decision-making
Championing governance, best practice, and responsible AI frameworks
Mentoring engineers and leading cross-functional delivery teams

This role offers a strong mix of strategy and hands-on engineering, with real influence over how AI is adopted across the organisation.


About You

You're an experienced AI/ML engineer with a strong track record of delivering production-ready solutions at scale. You're comfortable operating across both technical and business domains and enjoy turning complex ideas into practical outcomes.




You'll bring:





Strong background in software or data engineering (Python preferred)
Experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred) and Databricks ecosystem
Deep experience with ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn)
Solid MLOps capability, including model deployment and monitoring
Exposure to generative AI, LLMs, and modern AI frameworks (e.g. LangChain)
Proven ability to deliver AI solutions that drive measurable business value
Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership experience

Role Description:

The Senior Business Analyst is a 12 month fixed term contract in our Customer Business Unit based at our Central Park offices in Perth.

The Senior Business Analyst provides critical analytical expertise and insights to support the Customer Safeguard Program, enabling informed decision-making and efficient operational delivery. Working closely with program leads and cross-functional teams, the role ensures effective execution of customer safeguard, campaign planning, and workforce optimisation.

This position leads the analysis of customer payment data to identify trends and improvement opportunities, supporting the development of compliant, customer-focused solutions. Continuously build business analysis capability and share knowledge within the analysis community.
Drive process optimisation and contribute to best practice frameworks for business analysis.

Tertiary qualification in a related field (e.g. finance, economics, mathematics or data science)
5+ years' experience in business analysis within customer operations or Safeguard Programs.
Experience working with large datasets and reporting tools (e.g., Power BI, Excel, SQL)
Experience in energy, utilities, or regulated industries.

We are CBH. We are what you're looking for.





The Role

The Analyst/Senior Analyst - Investment & Planning plays a key role in guiding infrastructure investment decisions across our Storage and Handling network. This role blends strong commercial assessment and modelling with the ability to streamline complex issues, connect stakeholders and drive alignment, enabling prioritised, insight‑led decision making and capability uplift across Network Planning and the wider business.

Responsibilities include:

Develop and embed best‑practice processes, tools and templates, leveraging external insights to build internal capability and support consistent delivery of the Investment Plan

Partner with Area Managers, Operations Senior Leaders, Maintenance Superintendents and Terminal Managers to identify, assess and unlock high‑value opportunities

Undertake end‑to‑end strategic and commercial assessments for complex projects, including financial analysis, valuation and risk assessment

Job description:



The Pricing Strategy & Financial Performance Lead is an exciting opportunity in our Customer Marketing team based at our Perth Central Park office. This leadership role is critical to supporting Customer Business Unit by shaping pricing strategy, portfolio performance and financial outcomes.

You will lead a team of high performing analysts and partner closely with Sales, Products, Finance, and Trading teams to influence customer-centric decision making. Day to day you will be accountable for facilitating retail price modelling for products, retail portfolio forecasting, leading financial activities including budgeting, and guiding contestable pricing strategies across a complex and evolving market.

With a strong commercial lens, you will assess portfolio risks and opportunities, ensure robust pricing governance, and translate market, regulatory and financial analysis into clear, actionable insights for senior leaders.

This role suits a strategic minded leader who thrives with variety, dynamic environments, enjoys developing people, and is motivated by improving performance through data driven decision making, collaboration and innovation.

Job responsibilities:
Lead and develop a high performing Pricing Strategy and Financial Performance team, setting clear direction, role modelling positive culture, and ensuring delivery of high-quality commercial insights.
Drive long term financial planning for the Customer Business Unit, including demand, revenue and cost forecasting, budgeting and financial modelling, to support strategic decision making.
Define, implement and govern pricing strategies and methodologies across contestable markets and customer products, ensuring alignment with business objectives and market conditions.
Provide trusted commercial advice to senior stakeholders by analysing market, regulatory and financial impacts, identifying risks and opportunities, and translating complex data into clear, actionable insights.
Oversee portfolio and financial performance monitoring, including margin management, variance analysis and reporting, to support continuous improvement and sustainable financial outcomes.


Profile description:


Tertiary qualifications in Business, Finance, Economics or a related discipline, with strong commercial acumen and a risk aware mindset.
Proven people leadership experience, with the ability to lead, influence and develop high performing teams.
At least 3 years' experience in a senior analytics, pricing or commercial role, with demonstrated ability to interpret complex market and commercial drivers impacting profitability.
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with a track record of influencing decision making and presenting complex data clearly and concisely for non-technical audiences.
Highly developed analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience proactively proposing and driving initiatives that improve financial performance; experience with Power BI and tools such as Python or R is desirable.

Job Title: Guidewire Program Delivery Lead - Australia and New Zealand
Ad Text:
Tenzing is a leading Guidewire consultancy across Australia and New Zealand. A Guidewire Partner since 2007, we deliver complex, integrated insurance solutions for public and private organisations across both self‑managed platforms and Guidewire Cloud.
We were recognised as the Guidewire APAC Consulting Partner for Outstanding Market Growth (2023/2024). Our current work includes large‑scale Guidewire Cloud migrations, greenfield implementations, and long‑running DevOps delivery teams. As part of the Tech Mahindra Group, we combine strong local consulting expertise with global scale, supported by a Guidewire Centre of Excellence in India.

About the role:

We are seeking experienced Program Delivery Leads to join our delivery teams across Australia and New Zealand.

You will bring strong program leadership experience across Agile and hybrid delivery environments, with a proven ability to lead complex, multi‑stream transformation programmes. Hands‑on Guidewire delivery experience is advantageous but not essential.

This role is accountable for end‑to‑end program delivery, building high‑performing teams, and ensuring successful outcomes for both clients and delivery teams.


What You’ll Be Responsible For:


Providing end‑to‑end leadership for complex business and technology transformation programmes, including Guidewire implementations and enhancements.

Leading the delivery of large, multi‑stream programmes using Agile, SAFe, and hybrid delivery approaches.

Acting as a servant leader, creating a supportive environment that enables teams to perform at their best.

Providing day‑to‑day leadership for cross‑functional delivery teams, including developers, testers, business analysts, architects, and onshore/offshore resources.

Owning delivery planning, forecasting, and execution across sprints, releases, and milestonesActing as the primary point of accountability for delivery, proactively managing scope, dependencies, risks, and issues.

Building and maintaining strong, trusted relationships with client stakeholders, sponsors, and product owners to ensure alignment on priorities and outcomes.

Ensuring delivery aligns with agreed solution architecture, Guidewire best practices, and cloud standards.

Facilitating and governing Agile ceremonies (planning, reviews, retrospectives) and embedding effective ways of working.

Managing vendor, partner, and offshore delivery contributions to ensure seamless integration and performance.

Tracking and reporting delivery progress, financials, and risks, providing clear and transparent updates to senior stakeholders.

Championing continuous improvement, delivery excellence, team wellbeing, and strong governance across the programme




Experience & Background:

You will bring:

Proven program or senior delivery management experience within complex transformation environments.

A track record of successfully delivering large, multi‑stream programs using Agile, SAFe, or hybrid methodologies.

Strong leadership presence, with the ability to build and motivate high‑performing teams across diverse roles and backgrounds.

Excellent communication, negotiation, and risk management skills.

Experience delivering insurance or enterprise technology platforms (Guidewire experience is advantageous).




How You’ll Work:
At Tenzing, we value strong technical capability alongside excellent consulting and delivery skills. You’ll demonstrate:

Consulting and customer‑focused mindset - professional, clear communicator with a strong focus on delivering meaningful business outcomes and value for customers.

Agile delivery and collaboration - proven experience working in Scrum or SAFe environments, collaborating effectively with cross‑functional teams and stakeholders.

Clear communication and influence - able to explain complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.

Ownership, quality, and problem solving - takes accountability for outcomes, applies critical thinking to solve problems, and cares about building high‑quality, reliable, and maintainable solutions.

Adaptable and future‑focused - resilient in changing environments and proactively adopts AI‑enabled tools to improve individual and team productivity.


Why Join Tenzing?
For over twenty years, Tenzing has helped organisations navigate complex change. Our Sherpa ethos means standing alongside our clients, doing the hard work with them, and guiding them to successful outcomes.
As an independent subsidiary of the Tech Mahindra Group, we offer the best of both worlds - access to global expertise and opportunities, while maintaining our strong local culture and ways of working across New Zealand and Australia.
We invest in our people through tailored career development, mentoring, and a supportive, inclusive environment. Our leaders are experienced, approachable, and committed to helping our teams grow and thrive.


What We Offer:

Competitive salary

Discretionary annual bonus

Flexible and hybrid working arrangements

Option to purchase additional annual leave

Fully subsidised life and income protection insurance

Discounted medical insurance

Comprehensive learning and development programmes

Wellbeing initiatives including EAP and flu vaccinations

Inclusive culture with regular social events and celebrations


If you’re ready to work on meaningful Guidewire projects, grow your expertise, and build your career in a supportive consulting environment, we’d love to hear from you.

Job Title: Guidewire Business Analyst – Australia and New Zealand
Ad Text:
Tenzing is a leading Guidewire consultancy across Australia and New Zealand. A Guidewire Partner since 2007, we design and deliver complex, integrated insurance solutions for public and private organisations across both self‑managed platforms and Guidewire Cloud.

We were recognised as the
Guidewire APAC Consulting Partner for Outstanding Market Growth (2023/2024). Our current work includes large‑scale Guidewire Cloud migrations, greenfield implementations, and long‑running DevOps engagements. As part of the Tech Mahindra Group, we combine strong local consulting expertise with global scale, supported by our Guidewire Centre of Excellence in India.

About the role:

We are seeking Intermediate and Senior Guidewire Business Analysts to join our delivery teams across Australia and New Zealand.

You’ll bring strong, hands‑on Guidewire experience and a consulting mindset, working closely with clients and delivery teams to shape, translate, and deliver insurance solutions that meet real business outcomes. At Senior level, prior experience across insurance processes (policy, claims, or billing) is expected; at Intermediate level, this experience is highly desirable.


What You’ll Be Responsible For:

Working as a Business Analyst across both high‑level and detailed analysis, translating business outcomes and processes into clear, actionable requirements and user stories.

Leading the elicitation, analysis, and documentation of complex business requirements for Guidewire implementations and enhancements (PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, BillingCenter).

Acting as the key bridge between business stakeholders and delivery teams, translating business needs into functional specifications that support effective delivery.

Facilitating workshops, interviews, and discovery sessions with stakeholders to shape requirements, processes, and solution options.

Collaborating closely with product owners, architects, developers, and testers to ensure requirements are understood, feasible, and fit for purpose.

Supporting Agile delivery through backlog refinement, defining user stories and acceptance criteria, and contributing to sprint ceremonies.

Providing subject matter input across insurance processes (policy, claims, billing) and how these are enabled through Guidewire solutions.

Supporting solution design by validating requirements against Guidewire capabilities, configuration options, and best practices.

Identifying risks, gaps, and dependencies early, and working proactively with stakeholders to manage change

At Senior level, mentoring and supporting junior business analysts and contributing to standards and continuous improvement initiatives.

Guidewire & Domain Experience:

You will bring:

Hands‑on experience as a Business Analyst working on Guidewire implementations or enhancements.

Familiarity with Guidewire InsuranceSuite and Guidewire Cloud concepts.

Experience translating business processes into functional and user‑story‑based requirements.

Strong understanding of insurance domains (policy, claims, and/or billing), particularly at Senior level.

Relevant Guidewire certifications across one or more areas (desirable).


How You’ll Work:
At Tenzing, we value strong technical capability alongside excellent consulting and delivery skills. You’ll demonstrate:

Consulting and customer‑focused mindset - professional, clear communicator with a strong focus on delivering meaningful business outcomes and value for customers.

Agile delivery and collaboration - proven experience working in Scrum or SAFe environments, collaborating effectively with cross‑functional teams and stakeholders.

Clear communication and influence - able to explain complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.

Ownership, quality, and problem solving - takes accountability for outcomes, applies critical thinking to solve problems, and cares about building high‑quality, reliable, and maintainable solutions.

Adaptable and future‑focused - resilient in changing environments and proactively adopts AI‑enabled tools to improve individual and team productivity.

Why Join Tenzing?
For over twenty years, Tenzing has helped organisations navigate complex change. Our Sherpa ethos means standing alongside our clients, doing the hard work with them, and guiding them to successful outcomes.
As an independent subsidiary of the Tech Mahindra Group, we offer the best of both worlds - access to global expertise and opportunities, while maintaining our strong local culture and ways of working across New Zealand and Australia.
We invest in our people through tailored career development, mentoring, and a supportive, inclusive environment. Our leaders are experienced, approachable, and committed to helping our teams grow and thrive.


What We Offer:

Competitive salary

Discretionary annual bonus

Flexible and hybrid working arrangements

Option to purchase additional annual leave

Fully subsidised life and income protection insurance

Discounted medical insurance

Comprehensive learning and development programmes

Wellbeing initiatives including EAP and flu vaccinations

Inclusive culture with regular social events and celebrations


If you’re ready to work on meaningful Guidewire projects, grow your expertise, and build your career in a supportive consulting environment, we’d love to hear from you.

About Us

Adaptiv is a true Kiwi success story! Recognised for our commitment to premium client delivery, we provide world-class integration, AI consulting and development service, along with data and analytics. We have a wealth of development and integration experience in moving clients from around the world onto cloud platforms.




Our integration work focuses on the exciting challenge of designing innovative API-led and event driven solutions. Leveraging the latest technologies, such as Databricks, MuleSoft, Boomi, Azure, and Solace, we develop services that unlock access to existing data within legacy business systems in new and ingenious ways.




These solutions enable organizations to drive major process efficiencies, engineer new operational workflows, and extract deeper insights from their data. Ultimately, this empowers businesses to operate more effectively and engage with their customers in modern, cutting-edge ways. Our services also include cloud consultancy, round-the clock middleware support, integration development, application lifecycle management, load and performance testing and customised integration training.




Based in our stylish Southbank offices in Melbourne, our team enjoys a collaborative, people-first culture where curiosity, initiative, and growth are valued. We combine serious technical capability with a down-to-earth, can-do approach and we’re looking for someone who shares that mindset.





About the Role

We have an exciting opportunity for a Senior Data Engineer / Consultant to join our growing Data Engineering practice here at Adaptiv Integration to drive the delivery of data engineering engagements on behalf of our clients. In this role, you will ensure high-quality technical outcomes, foster strong client collaboration, and provide effective support to delivery teams.




This position combines advanced data engineering expertise with consulting leadership. You will own engineering delivery across assigned engagements, contribute to solution design within architectural guidelines, and mentor junior and intermediate team members to maintain consistent quality and standards.




This role will be responsible for the delivery of cutting-edge Databricks technology, driving innovative data engineering solutions for clients. You will leverage your expertise to design, implement, and optimise Databricks-based pipelines and solutions, ensuring high-quality technical outcomes and efficient, scalable data architectures.




The role is highly hands-on and delivery-focused, requiring autonomous execution and a strong emphasis on billable contribution.




What You’ll Be Doing

Build, maintain, and optimise data pipelines across lakes, lakehouses, and warehouses.
Write, test, and maintain high-quality code; develop transformations for reporting, analytics, and AI/ML.
Apply data modelling principles; troubleshoot and resolve issues efficiently.
Work with Databricks, Delta Live Tables, Lakeflow, Git, and CI/CD pipelines.
Implement automated testing, data validation, and performance optimisation.
Lead unit and integration testing; ensure solutions meet quality and documentation standards.
Support knowledge sharing and improvement of internal frameworks and templates.
Act as primary engineering contact, leading technical discussions and translating business needs into technical tasks.
Communicate progress, risks, and blockers; contribute to estimation, scoping, and planning.
Mentor and coach junior/intermediate team members; conduct peer reviews.
Ensure accountability for code quality, documentation, and adherence to best practices.
Collaborate with architects on technical solutions and delivery planning.
Participate in learning sessions and knowledge sharing; adopt modern data concepts (AI/ML, data visualisation, cloud-native).
Contribute to process, template, and documentation improvements; pursue relevant training and certification.




What You’ll Bring

6+ years of professional experience in data engineering or a related field.

At least 4 years of hands-on experience developing data pipelines and transformations using Databricks, including Delta Live Tables, notebooks, and workflows.

Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric concepts and experience contributing to projects that incorporate Fabric components.

Proficiency in Python and SQL for data transformation and analysis.
Understanding of data modelling including Star and Snowflake schema design for analytics workloads.
Knowledge of Medallion architecture principles and well-architected, scalable data solution design.

Experience applying DevOps practices such as version control, CI/CD, and environment automation.

Experience with testing and data quality frameworks to ensure reliable, accurate data delivery.

Experience with visualisation and data consumption concepts, including Power BI or similar tools.


Relevant industry certifications (Databricks Professional and Databricks Associate) or a demonstrated commitment to ongoing professional development.

 

If you are looking for your next role with an organisation where you can elevate your career, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

Ad Text:
Develop your career with Sunshine Coast Health in southeast Queensland which extends through the coastal and hinterland areas from Caloundra in the south and Gympie in the north.

We're excited to welcome a Principal Data Analyst – Scientific Analysis, who performs applied analytical investigations to inform operational and clinical decision-making across Sunshine Coast Hospital and Health Service.

The role works with clinicians, managers and executives to clarify problems, translate them into structured analytical approaches, and deliver evidence-based findings that support action.

Sunshine Coast Health is a values-based organisation. We are committed to the maintenance of a culture in which we treat our colleagues with respect as we do our patients, consumers and the community. We respect, protect and promote human rights in our decision-making and actions.

Key Responsibilities include:

Engage with clinicians, managers and stakeholders to clarify operational and clinical problems and translate them into structured analytical approaches and work plans, for example understanding drivers of overcrowding, delays to discharge or variation in care pathways.
Plan and lead defined analytical work packages and small projects, managing scope, timelines and deliverables within a busy operational environment.
Undertake complex data extraction, preparation and analysis using SQL and analytical programming languages such as R or Python.

Remuneration value up to $171,568 p.a., comprising salary between $140,115 - $150,371 p.a., employer contribution to superannuation (12.75%) and annual leave loading (17.5%) (iAO7). Applications will remain current for 12 months. Please note: only applications from candidates will be accepted (applications that may result in an agency fee will not be considered).

Why work for us?

We offer rewarding career opportunities across a wide range of clinical and non-clinical areas across our health service and pride ourselves on providing a work environment that is safe, satisfying, flexible, and promotes a healthy work-life balance.

As a Queensland Health employee, you will benefit from a higher than standard employer contribution to Superannuation of up to 12.75%, access to salary packaging, flexible working arrangements and competitive salary rates with annual incremental increases.

We are committed to providing a diverse and inclusive workplace for our people and our community. We encourage people of all genders, races, ages and abilities to apply for roles within our Health Service.

You can find out more about why it's so great to work at Sunshine Coast here: https://www.health.qld.gov.au/sunshinecoast/careers

As a Sunshine Coast Health employee, you will receive competitive remuneration and have access to:

A wellness program;
Generous superannuation;
Flexible work arrangements;
Career training and development; and
Salary packaging.

Check out the latest updates here:

https://www.sunshinecoast.health.qld.gov.au/

Who are we?
Landpower and the CLAAS Harvest Centre network is the largest privately-owned farm machinery distribution network in Australasia. We sell and service machinery to farmers and their contractors from our locations throughout New Zealand and Australia.

Our Christchurch and Melbourne offices co-ordinate wholesale activities and provide support functions. As a family-owned business we recognise the importance and value of the long-term relationships that we have grown over the last 45 years with our international partners.

What’s the job?
This is a varied and commercially focused role supporting our Product Management, Sales and Pricing teams across Australia and New Zealand. Reporting to the Group Product Controller, you will provide analytical support across sales order planning, pricing, CRM reporting, market analysis and sales programs.
You will work closely with Product Managers, Regional Sales Managers, Pricing and Costing, Logistics, Finance and Technology teams to ensure high‑quality insights inform business decisions.

Key deliverables of the role will include:

Supporting the monthly Sales Order Planning (SOP) process, including supply and demand modelling by product, region and supplier
Preparing sales, stock, pipeline and lost‑sales reporting to support ordering and allocation decisions
Maintains market analysis for Australia and New Zealand
Supporting CRM analytics, KPI dashboards and data integrity across the sales organisation
Assisting with the development, execution and monitoring of sales programs
Supporting retail machine pricing activities, including pricing updates, analysis and scenario modelling
Preparing reporting for suppliers and external partners
Actively contributing to Group objectives and supporting colleagues as required

Who are you?

You have 2+ years’ experience in a commercial, product, sales, pricing or business analyst role
Experience in pricing, sales planning, inventory or market analysis is highly regarded
Experience within the agriculture industry is highly desirable
You hold a Bachelor’s degree in business, agriculture, supply chain or a related discipline
You have advanced Excel skills, with Power BI experience highly regarded
You are comfortable working with CRM and ERP systems, data models and dashboards
You are highly analytical, detail‑focused and confident managing competing priorities
You communicate clearly and can present insights to a broad range of stakeholders
You enjoy working collaboratively and supporting continuous improvement
  
To be successful in the role you must be eligible to live and work in Australia.

We are looking for people who share our values!
In It Together, Front Up, and Keep Growing form the backbone of what is a dynamic business, providing a challenging and stimulating environment. Family is key to us – We’re a family-owned business, we employ people with families to care for, and our purpose is to provide resources and support to families in the farming community to enable their success as they feed our world.

We are looking for people who share our values if this sounds like you - apply today!

Job Title: Expression of Interest - Executive Advisor - Commercial WA
Ad Text:
At GHD, we don’t just believe in the power of commitment, we live and breathe it every day.

 

GHD’s Infrastructure Advisory practice, a team of > 400 people across ANZ, South-east Asia and the Pacific, supports Government, asset managers and infrastructure investors who make informed decisions about capital deployment, operational optimisation and liability transformation. 

Our team is committed to solving complex challenges across the value chain, from capital deployment to asset management, enabling resilience, growth and sustainability. 

Together, we will help create a future for generations to come.

 

Who are we looking for?

 

We are seeking expressions of interest for Perth-based Executive Advisors familiar with areas like business case development, economic / financial modelling, project advisory, public policy, strategy and regulation as we expand our Government and Commercial Advisory team in WA. 

Based in our Perth Office, your role will be to lead key client relationships and engagements that shape Australia’s infrastructure policy and associated major projects.

Working with a high performing team, this position offers a variety of work and will see you involved in:

Using your expertise and knowledge to successfully win and deliver business cases, economic and financial modelling, and public policy and strategy work 
Project pursuits including internal teaming arrangements and contractual negotiations
Owning and delivering a variety of projects at all levels.
Establishing and maintaining effective relationships with clients and collaborating with internal GHD regions and Leadership teams 
Providing technical leadership on nominated projects as required

 

What would you bring to the team? 

 

Over 10 years’ experience delivering Business Advisory services 
Proven experience delivering Government business cases, economic and financial modelling and policy development.
You will have proven experience in working on business development and relationship building. 
Commercial acumen and critical-thinking ability 
Strong report writing, interviewing, facilitation and presentation skills.
Ability to influence others, negotiating skills.

 

GHD Advisory – clear, actionable advice that is anchored in deep technical knowledge and drives our clients’ success.

 

Interested? Express your interest now!

 

GHD is an equal opportunity employer, and we want everyone to be able to fully participate in our recruitment process. We value a diverse workforce and an inclusive culture. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people of all identities.

GHD embraces a range of flexible work practices to create a work environment focused on well-being, flexibility and productivity where our people can thrive. 

Be part of the dynamic culture! Our workplace thrives on a hybrid model, combining in-office collaboration and flexibility. Join us in office, three days a week to contribute, connect and excel in our vibrant environment.

#LI-MG1

Start May with more engaging sustainability projects, a pay rise and the most exciting workplace in Perth. 

Super Smart Energy is a new kind of consultancy. We are founder-led and bring together deep expertise in Energy and Sustaniability solutions, to deliver client value, and drive the Sustainability agenda. We do this by combining world leading technology and data, to guide strategy and transform business decision making by implementing sustainable solutions that deliver significant cost benefits. Our Office is located in West Perth, we have a passionate team that is focused on long-term relationships with our clients and delivering significant value to them.

Our Culture

We attract and retain top tier talent from business analytics, engineering and management consulting by providing outstanding career opportunities. We actively promote a work-life balance. We have a genuine flexible work policy, and many of our team work regularly from home when needed.

The Challenge

· Do you want to be a key part of a team that is guiding large corporates to reduce carbon emissions?

· Are you keen to use your skills to help transform businesses and create good in the world?

· Are you ready to work with a leading Managing Director (MD) to ensure that together, you can fulfil the outstanding potential of this rapidly growing and dynamic company?

If you answered YES to the above, then we have that once in a generation role for you.

The Role

As a full time Senior Carbon Analyst, you will support the business by providing in depth analysis and recommendations to clients on Carbon and ESG related issues. Your attention to detail and due diligence will set you apart from the rest. The ability to use the data to make commercial decisions is a must.

About you

We are hoping you can bring the following:

Related tertiary qualifications: Commerce, Engineering, Finance, Economics, Legal, or a related discipline.

· 5+ years of industry experience (a must), ideally with sustainability audit experience.

· Carbon Accounting or Sustainability Consulting experience, ideally with a large Tier 1 Mining Company or a large consulting firm.

· Proficiency in Financial Analysis, GHG Assessments (Scope 1,2 & 3 (must have completed multiple Scope 3 assessments)), AASB S2 Sustainability Reporting, Sustainability Analysis, Data Management & Report Writing.

· Team player, you should be an adept communicator with fantastic negotiation and stakeholder engagement skills.

· Entrepreneurial strategic thinker, collaborate and embrace change.

· Wide knowledge of industry and solutions to decarbonize.

· Expertise in NGERs, NPI, AASB S2, IFRS and Supply Chain Emissions.

· Ability to build rapport, manage relationships and negotiate.

· Strong analytical skills and detail-oriented.

· Confident and self-aware with excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal.

· Advanced user of Excel and PowerPoint.

Salary

Extremely competitive salary, based on skills and experience. Starting at $120,000 plus superannuation a year. Equity earn in available for the right candidate.

Application process

To apply for the role, please submit the following:

· A cover letter detailing why you would make a great fit for the role; and

· Your resume.

Please address your application to info@supersmart.energy.

We look forward to hearing from you soon.




Please dont submit an application if you havent completed multiple scope 3 assessments as you wont be considered for the role.

·         Lead the research agenda for a university with globally recognised research strengths and a bold new Strategic Plan

·         Shape a research culture and support environment that is ambitious, inclusive and built for the future

·         Drive transformational change in how UNE enables its research community to thrive

The University of New England is setting out to do things differently. This role is no exception. Regionally rooted but globally networked, UNE is home to research that matters — knowledge that connects regional Australia to national and global opportunity, and that supports individuals and communities to thrive. With a new Strategic Plan setting an ambitious direction, this is an exciting moment to join us.

The Role

As Director of Research, you will shape and sustain the strategic and operational effectiveness of UNE's research support environment. Reporting to the Deputy Vice-Chancellor Research (DVC-R), you will provide authoritative counsel on research strategy, policy, governance and compliance, while leading a directorate that is collaborative, responsive and continuously improving.

This is a role that exists to remove barriers, build confidence and create the conditions in which UNE's researchers can do their best work — from early-career academics finding their feet, to established research leaders pursuing ambitious programs.

It is also a role for someone who can lead through change. UNE is in the midst of an operating model transition, and the Director of Research will be expected to actively contribute to new ways of working — embracing simplification, AI-enabled workflows and capability uplift as genuine priorities, not afterthoughts.

What You'll Lead

·         Research strategy development and implementation in partnership with the DVC-R, including shaping institutional research priorities and managing the Research Performance Framework

·         A capable, service-focused Research Services directorate

·         The full research development lifecycle. From grant identification and application support through to reporting, acquittal and post-award management

·         A research culture that is ambitious, inclusive and well-supported, including mentoring early-career researchers and building capability across the institution

·         External partnerships with funding bodies, government agencies, industry and peer institutions, with particular focus on opportunities of regional significance

·         Institutional leadership on research governance, integrity, ethics, compliance, export controls and foreign interference obligations

·         Operational excellence. Reducing administrative burden and improving the researcher experience

·         Thoughtful integration of AI and digital tools to improve research support outcomes

What You'll Bring

·         Significant experience in research leadership, development and administration at a senior level, with a track record of building institutional research capacity and culture

·         A doctoral qualification (preferred) or equivalent senior experience in research management and strategy

·         Deep understanding of the Australian research funding landscape, regulatory environment and compliance obligations

·         Demonstrated ability to lead diverse teams, foster collaboration across academic and professional boundaries, and build productive external partnerships

·         Strategic and entrepreneurial thinking in research support — identifying opportunities, managing risk and driving continuous improvement

·         An appreciation for data-informed research management, including familiarity with research performance frameworks or researcher productivity systems

·         Highly developed communication, relationship-building and influencing skills, with the ability to operate credibly across all levels of the institution and with external stakeholders

·         A genuine capacity to contribute to organisational change, including the adoption of AI-enabled tools and evolving governance frameworks

If you're energised by the prospect of building something genuinely better, in an institution with the ambition and appetite to match, we'd love to hear from you.

Why UNE?

UNE has always punched above its weight, in research quality, in regional impact, and in its willingness to do things differently. This is a rare opportunity to help write the next chapter: a research environment that is ambitious, modern and genuinely enabling — for a leader who acts with courage, builds relationships that last, and delivers results that matter.

About the University of New England

The University of New England (UNE) is a regionally based, globally networked university renowned for the quality of its student experience and the excellence of its research specialisations. It stands as a beacon of innovation, with a legacy stretching back to the 1950s when it became Australia’s first provider of distance learning. Today, UNE continues to redefine remote learning through cutting-edge programs and a deeply student-centred approach.

UNE’s education model combines unrivalled experience with modern, flexible delivery, ideal for students juggling life, work, or remote locations. With a library of over 200 courses, robust support systems, and a recognised track record for quality, UNE offers more than access to education, it offers a future-focused learning journey designed for success in a rapidly evolving world.

UNE emphasises its people, staff and students' growth and development. We aspire to be a leader in innovation and change and a centre for the Armidale and the North-West communities.

About the Role

Dentons currently has an exciting opportunity for an innovative and detail-oriented Business Analyst to join our team on a 12-month contract.

As a Business Analyst, you will be responsible for enhancing the quality of our technology products by analysing business needs and opportunities. You will work closely with Organisational Change Management and Technical SMEs to design improvements, facilitate business testing, and support implementation. This role will see you working on Dentons' Case Management System project, with experience in ShareDo (Clio Operate), Appian, or other CRMs highly regarded.

With no single headquarters, Dentons offers an environment where different perspectives and innovation are encouraged. You will play a key part in ensuring that our projects are built on a solid foundation of well-documented and thoroughly analysed requirements.

Your Key Duties Will Include:

Requirements gathering sessions to capture business needs, including risk identification in partnership with project managers.

Preparing and maintaining all collateral relating to business requirements throughout the project lifecycle, including ensuring stakeholder sign-off.

Collaborating with project managers, stakeholders and business units to document and analyse business requirements for projects.

Ensuring comprehensive requirements traceability using Requirements Traceability and Testing Matrices (RTTM).

Translating business needs into clear specifications and project documentation including business cases, use cases, and process flows.

Working with project managers to develop and implement plans for successful project delivery, improvements and new solutions.

Conducting process impact assessments, feasibility studies and gap analysis to inform project planning and execution.

Participate in testing with particular emphasis on UAT, Business Pre & Post Implementation Verification.

Process mapping with focus on automation

Maintain and enhance documentation repositories and project tools (e.g., project plans, dashboards, knowledge bases).

What You'll Need to Succeed

Min 3-5 years' experience as a Business Analyst or similar role.

Experience working in the legal industry

Agile Business Analyst, BABOK or related Certification.

User Acceptance, Regression and Business Verification Testing.

Proficiency in a variety of Project tools/systems (e.g. Microsoft Excel, Smartsheet, PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams, PowerBI, Jira/Confluence etc).

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to foster relationships across all levels of the business.

Proven analytical mindset, capable of solving complex problems and presenting actionable solutions.
